title: CEO Interview with Mark Goranson of EMASS - Semiwiki
gist: Daniel Nenni interviews EMASS CEO Mark Goranson (a Nanoveu subsidiary) on its event-driven, ultra-low-power edge-AI SoC platform ECS-DoT, which keeps devices dormant until meaningful sensor activity, runs always-on inference on-device within a sub-milliwatt envelope, and targets wearables, drones, and industrial IoT where battery life is the binding constraint.

insights.claim: EMASS's ECS-DoT is an event-driven SoC that keeps devices in an ultra-low-power state until meaningful sensor activity is detected and runs always-on AI inference entirely on-chip, removing the need for cloud inference or frequent external-memory access.
insights.speaker: Mark Goranson
insights.so_what: Event-driven gating plus on-chip inference is the differentiator versus duty-cycled processors; a design pattern to watch as edge-AI silicon competes on milliwatts.
insights.quote: Its event-driven system-on-chip architecture keeps devices in an ultra-low-power state until meaningful sensor activity is detected, reducing unnecessary computation and energy use.

insights.claim: ECS-DoT can run continuous AI inference within a sub-milliwatt power envelope, which Goranson says very few processors can match despite many being able to run AI at all.
insights.speaker: Mark Goranson
insights.so_what: Sub-milliwatt always-on inference is the claimed moat; verify against incumbents to gauge whether EMASS opens previously impractical battery-constrained use cases.
insights.quote: Many processors can run AI, but very few can do so continuously within a sub-milliwatt power envelope.
insights.locator: What does the competitive landscape look like...

insights.claim: EMASS targets battery-powered systems needing continuous sensing: wearables (smart glasses, hearables, fitness trackers), drones/robotics, and industrial IoT/smart infrastructure where transmitting only meaningful events reduces bandwidth and extends battery life.
insights.speaker: Mark Goranson
insights.so_what: Maps the addressable verticals where ultra-low-power edge AI wins; battery life is the recurring buying trigger across all of them.
insights.quote: ECS-DoT delivers the greatest value in battery-powered systems requiring continuous sensing and real-time intelligence within strict energy constraints.
insights.locator: What application areas are your strongest?

insights.claim: One EMASS use case uses bone-conduction sensing through an IMU to support keyword spotting without continuously active microphones, improving both power efficiency and privacy.
insights.speaker: Mark Goranson
insights.so_what: Concrete mic-less wake-word approach illustrates the privacy + power angle that differentiates always-on wearable AI.
insights.quote: One use case uses bone-conduction sensing through an IMU to support keyword spotting without continuously active microphones, improving power efficiency and privacy.
insights.locator: What application areas are your strongest?

insights.claim: Goranson positions a new capability: using on-device AI not just to interpret sensor data but to control device operation itself, e.g. significantly improving a drone's flight dynamics.
insights.speaker: Mark Goranson
insights.so_what: Shifting AI from perception to actuation/control expands the value proposition and the addressable BOM per device.
insights.quote: with our system we’re also making it practical to use AI for the operation of the device itself.
insights.locator: What keeps your customers up at night?

insights.claim: EMASS's roadmap spans three tracks: scaling power/performance on more advanced process nodes with multi-modal (vision+audio+motion) always-on workloads, investing in developer experience (model zoo, toolkit/IDE, framework support), and deepening sensor/distributor/toolchain partnerships.
insights.speaker: Mark Goranson
insights.so_what: Developer-experience and ecosystem investment signals that adoption friction, not raw silicon, is the gating factor for edge-AI chip vendors.
insights.quote: we’re putting real investment into the developer experience: an expanding model zoo of ready-to-deploy models, a toolkit and IDE that shorten the path from a customer’s data to a model running on the chip
insights.locator: What new features/technology are you working on?

insights.claim: Customers engage almost always via an evaluation/developer-kit first, benchmarking power against their target before moving to a design-in phase, proof-of-concept, integration, and commercial terms.
insights.speaker: Mark Goranson
insights.so_what: Eval-kit-led sales motion means design-win conversion timing is the metric to track for revenue ramp.
insights.quote: Almost always through an evaluation first. We get a developer kit into the customer’s hands so they can run ECS-DoT in their own environment, benchmark the power against their target
insights.locator: How do customers normally engage with your company?
